page: Microsoft Azure L64s_v2 is an instance from the LSv2 family, featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7551 processor running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, this server provides dedicated CPU allocation with 2.0 threads per core. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes a substantial local storage capacity of 16015 GB and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not feature any GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals distinct performance characteristics. Single-core performance is a notable bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10% and bottom 25% categories respectively. Similarly, single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show weak performance. However, the server leverages its 64 vCPUs to achieve average multi-core performance, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression benchmarks. Memory bandwidth remains stable in the middle 50% tier, with read-only large block speeds at 109.02 GB/sec and write speeds at 51.59 GB/sec. Notably, the hardware performs strongly in cryptographic operations, scoring in the top 25% for the PassMark CPU encryption test.

The primary tradeoff of the L64s_v2 lies in its low single-threaded execution speed contrasted against its high storage density and balanced multi-core throughput. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the massive 16015 GB local storage combined with 512 GB of RAM offers high resource density for data-intensive workloads without the premium cost of specialized GPU hardware. This profile makes the instance suitable for large-scale database operations, Redis deployments, and static web serving, where parallel processing and high storage capacity are prioritized over raw single-core compute speed. It is less suitable for complex LLM text generation or single-threaded compilation tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure L64s_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server within the LSv2 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7551 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores. It features 512.0 GB of RAM and a massive 16015 GB of local storage, supporting Premium Storage. While single-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench single-core, fall into the lower percentiles, the server delivers average multi-core performance and strong cryptographic execution, as evidenced by its top-tier PassMark encryption score. With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high storage density, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for storage-heavy and memory-intensive workloads. It is well-suited for database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving, though it presents performance tradeoffs for single-threaded tasks and advanced LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L64s_v2 is an LSv2-family server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 16015 GB local storage, offering average multi-core performance and strong cryptography for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L64s_v2 features 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 16015 GB local storage, optimized for database and memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory-optimized server featuring sixty-four virtual cores and over sixteen terabytes of local storage.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7551 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
512 GB of system memory paired with 16015 GB of local storage
Strong cryptographic performance in PassMark encryption benchmarks
Average multi-core processing and memory bandwidth capabilities
Tradeoffs include weak single-core and single-threaded performance
Best suited for large-scale database operations and Redis deployments
